commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces chained `as unknown as` casts in `src/cron/service/store.ts` with direct annotations while keeping cron store normalization, validation, and quarantine flow unchanged.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a cleanup PR rather than a user-visible failing behavior. The contributor did provide before/after cron status terminal output for the touched runtime path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because the PR has no actionable patch defect; normal maintainer and CI review can decide landing.

target.title: refactor(cron): reduce excessive `as unknown as` type assertions in s…
target.body:
# Summary
Replaces excessive as unknown as type assertions in src/cron/service/store.ts with direct type annotations.
CronStoreFile.jobs is already typed as CronJob[], so the intermediate cast through unknown is unnecessary.
CronJob extends CronJobBase which is an object type, so it satisfies Record<string, unknown> without an intermediate cast.
No behavioral changes — purely removing unsafe type assertions while preserving all validation logic.

# Linked context
Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/91314

# Real behavior proof (after-fix evidence from real OpenClaw setup)
### Test Environment
- Instance: Local OpenClaw
- OS: Linux x86_64
- Node.js: v24.16.0
- Running subsystem: Cron service

### Before Fix
Execute command:
openclaw cron status
Output:
{
  "enabled": true,
  "storePath": "/root/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json",
  "jobs": 1,
  "nextWakeAtMs": 1735445760000
}
Result: Cron store loads normally, service runs without errors.

### After Fix
Applied code changes and restarted cron service, execute the same command:
openclaw cron status
Output:
{
  "enabled": true,
  "storePath": "/root/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json",
  "jobs": 1,
  "nextWakeAtMs": 1735445760000
}
Result:
- No runtime errors thrown
- Cron store loads successfully
- All original functions work normally
- Output is completely consistent with before the fix

Conclusion: This change only cleans up redundant type assertions, no functional or behavioral changes.

# Tests and validation
1. Type check: npx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json → No TypeScript errors
2. Unit tests: All cron store test cases passed
> node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/service/store.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/seed-test/open-claw/openclaw

 ✓  cron  src/cron/service/store.test.ts (10 tests) 477ms
     ✓ loads stored jobs, recomputes next runs, and does not rewrite the store on load  379ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
   Start at  14:32:13
   Duration  15.80s (transform 12.23s, setup 1.88s, import 12.92s, tests 477ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 29.03s

# Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? No
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area: None
Risk mitigation: All real environment verification and test cases passed.

# Current review state
Next action: Ready for maintainer review
target.author: specialleon
